documentation:
# CrateDB Python Client

```{image} https://github.com/crate/crate-python/workflows/Tests/badge.svg
:alt: Build status
:target: https://github.com/crate/crate-python/actions?workflow=Tests
```

```{image} https://codecov.io/gh/crate/crate-python/branch/main/graph/badge.svg
:alt: Coverage
:target: https://app.codecov.io/gh/crate/crate-python
```

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/crate-python/badge/
:alt: Build status (documentation)
:target: https://cratedb.com/docs/python/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/crate.svg
:alt: PyPI Version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/crate/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/pyversions/crate.svg
:alt: Python Version
:target: https://pypi.org/project/crate/
```

```{image} https://static.pepy.tech/badge/crate/month
:alt: PyPI Downloads
:target: https://pepy.tech/project/crate
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/wheel/crate.svg
:alt: Wheel
:target: https://pypi.org/project/crate/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/status/crate.svg
:alt: Status
:target: https://pypi.org/project/crate/
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/pypi/l/crate.svg
:alt: License
:target: https://pypi.org/project/crate/
```

A Python client library for [CrateDB], implementing the Python [DB API 2.0]
specification.

The CrateDB dialect for [SQLAlchemy] is provided by the [sqlalchemy-cratedb]
package, see also [sqlalchemy-cratedb documentation].

## Installation

The CrateDB Python client is available as package [crate] on [PyPI].

To install the most recent driver version, run:

```
$ pip install --upgrade crate
```

## Migration Notes

If you are migrating from previous versions of `crate[sqlalchemy]<1.0.0`, you
will find that the newer releases `crate>=1.0.0` no longer include the
SQLAlchemy dialect for CrateDB.

See [migrate to sqlalchemy-cratedb] for relevant guidelines about how to
successfully migrate to the [sqlalchemy-cratedb] package.

## Documentation and Help

- [CrateDB Python Client documentation]
- [CrateDB reference documentation]
- [Developer documentation]
- [Contributing]
- Other [support channels]

## Contributions

The CrateDB Python client library is an open source project, and is [managed on
GitHub][managed on github]. We appreciate contributions of any kind.

[contributing]: CONTRIBUTING.rst
[crate]: https://pypi.org/project/crate/
[crate.io]: https://cratedb.com/
[cratedb]: https://github.com/crate/crate
[cratedb python client documentation]: https://cratedb.com/docs/python/
[cratedb reference documentation]: https://crate.io/docs/reference/
[db api 2.0]: https://peps.python.org/pep-0249/
[developer documentation]: DEVELOP.rst
[managed on github]: https://github.com/crate/crate-python
[migrate to sqlalchemy-cratedb]: https://cratedb.com/docs/sqlalchemy-cratedb/migrate-from-crate-client.html
[pypi]: https://pypi.org/
[sqlalchemy]: https://www.sqlalchemy.org/
[sqlalchemy-cratedb]: https://github.com/crate/sqlalchemy-cratedb
[sqlalchemy-cratedb documentation]: https://cratedb.com/docs/sqlalchemy-cratedb/
[stackoverflow]: https://stackoverflow.com/tags/cratedb
[support channels]: https://cratedb.com/support/

classes.name: crate.client.blob.BlobContainer

methods.name: crate.client.blob.BlobContainer.put
documentation:
Upload a blob

```
param f

File object to be uploaded (required to support seek if digest is
not provided).

param digest

Optional SHA-1 hex digest of the file contents. Gets computed
before actual upload if not provided, which requires an extra file
read.

return

The hex digest of the uploaded blob if not provided in the call.
Otherwise a boolean indicating if the blob has been newly created.
```

methods.name: crate.client.blob.BlobContainer.get
documentation:
Return the contents of a blob

```
param digest

the hex digest of the blob to return

param chunk_size

the size of the chunks returned on each iteration

return

generator returning chunks of data
```

methods.name: crate.client.blob.BlobContainer.delete
documentation:
Delete a blob

```
param digest

the hex digest of the blob to be deleted

return

True if blob existed
```

methods.name: crate.client.blob.BlobContainer.exists
documentation:
Check if a blob exists

```
param digest

Hex digest of the blob

return

Boolean indicating existence of the blob
```

class that represents a blob collection in crate.

can be used to download, upload and delete blobs

classes.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.execute
documentation: Prepare and execute a database operation (query or command).

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.executemany
documentation:
Prepare a database operation (query or command) and then execute it
against all parameter sequences or mappings found in the sequence
`seq_of_parameters`.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.fetchone
documentation:
Fetch the next row of a query result set, returning a single sequence,
or None when no more data is available.
Alias for `next()`.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.__iter__
documentation:
support iterator interface:
http://legacy.python.org/dev/peps/pep-0249/#iter

This iterator is shared. Advancing this iterator will advance other
iterators created from this cursor.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.fetchmany
documentation:
Fetch the next set of rows of a query result, returning a sequence of
sequences (e.g. a list of tuples). An empty sequence is returned when
no more rows are available.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.fetchall
documentation:
Fetch all (remaining) rows of a query result, returning them as a
sequence of sequences (e.g. a list of tuples). Note that the cursor's
arraysize attribute can affect the performance of this operation.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.close
documentation: Close the cursor now

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.setinputsizes
documentation: Not supported method.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.setoutputsize
documentation: Not supported method.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.rowcount
documentation:
This read-only attribute specifies the number of rows that the last
.execute\*() produced (for DQL statements like `SELECT`) or affected
(for DML statements like `UPDATE` or `INSERT`).

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.next
documentation:
Return the next row of a query result set, respecting if cursor was
closed.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.description
documentation: This read-only attribute is a sequence of 7-item sequences.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.duration
documentation:
This read-only attribute specifies the server-side duration of a query
in milliseconds.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor._convert_rows
documentation: Iterate rows, apply type converters, and generate converted rows.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.time_zone
documentation: Get the current time zone.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.time_zone
documentation:
Set the time zone.

Different data types are supported. Available options are:

- `datetime.timezone.utc`
- `datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=7), name="MST")`
- `pytz.timezone("Australia/Sydney")`
- `zoneinfo.ZoneInfo("Australia/Sydney")`
- `+0530` (UTC offset in string format)

The driver always returns timezone-"aware" `datetime` objects,
with their `tzinfo` attribute set.

When `time_zone` is `None`, the returned `datetime` objects are
using Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), because CrateDB is storing
timestamp values in this format.

When `time_zone` is given, the timestamp values will be transparently
converted from UTC to use the given time zone.

methods.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor._timezone_from_utc_offset
documentation: UTC offset in string format (e.g. `+0530`) to `datetime.timezone`.

class_variables.name: crate.client.cursor.Cursor.lastrowid
documentation: currently not supported

not thread-safe by intention
should not be shared between different threads

classes.name: crate.testing.layer.CrateLayer

methods.name: crate.testing.layer.CrateLayer.from_uri
documentation:
Download the Crate tarball from a URI and create a CrateLayer

```
param uri

The uri that points to the Crate tarball

param name

layer and cluster name

param http_port

The http port on which Crate will listen

param transport_port

the transport port on which Crate will listen

param settings

A dictionary that contains Crate settings

param directory

Where the tarball will be extracted to.
If this is None a temporary directory will be created.

param clean

a boolean indicating if the directory should be removed
on teardown.

param verbose

Set the log verbosity of the test layer
```

methods.name: crate.testing.layer.CrateLayer.__init__
documentation:
---
param cluster_name: |-
  the name of the cluster to join/build. Will be
  generated automatically if omitted.
param crate_config: |-
  alternative crate config file location.
  Must be a directory or a file named 'crate.yml'
param crate_exec: alternative executable command
param crate_home: path to home directory of the crate installation
param env: Set environment variables.
param host: the host to bind to. defaults to 'localhost'
param name: layer name, is also used as the cluser name
param port: port on which crate should run
param settings: |-
  further settings that do not deserve a keyword
  argument will be prefixed with `es.`.
param transport_port: |-
  port on which transport layer for crate should
  run
param verbose: Set the log verbosity of the test layer
---

documentation: Wait for instance to be started

This layer starts a Crate server.

methods.name: crate.client.connection.Connection.__init__
documentation:
---
param backoff_factor: |-
  (optional)
  define the retry interval for unreachable servers in seconds
param ca_cert: |-
  a path to a CA certificate to use when verifying the SSL server
  certificate.
param cert_file: a path to the client certificate to present to the server.
param client: |-
  (optional - for testing)
  client used to communicate with crate.
param converter: |-
  (optional, defaults to `None`)
  A `Converter` object to propagate to newly created `Cursor` objects.
param error_trace: |-
  if set to `True` return a whole stacktrace of any server error if
  one occurs
param key_file: a path to the client key to use when communicating with the server.
param password: the password of the user in the database.
param pool_size: |-
  (optional)
  Number of connections to save that can be reused.
  More than 1 is useful in multithreaded situations.
param servers: |-
  either a string in the form of '\<hostname>:\<port>'
  or a list of servers in the form of ['\<hostname>:\<port>', '...']
param socket_keepalive: |-
  (optional, defaults to `True`)
  Enable TCP keepalive on socket level.
param socket_tcp_keepcnt: |-
  (optional)
  Set the `TCP_KEEPCNT` socket option, which overrides
  `net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_probes` kernel setting if `socket_keepalive`
  is `True`.
param socket_tcp_keepidle: |-
  (optional)
  Set the `TCP_KEEPIDLE` socket option, which overrides
  `net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_time` kernel setting if `socket_keepalive`
  is `True`.
param socket_tcp_keepintvl: |-
  (optional)
  Set the `TCP_KEEPINTVL` socket option, which overrides
  `net.ipv4.tcp_keepalive_intvl` kernel setting if `socket_keepalive`
  is `True`.
param time_zone: |-
  (optional, defaults to `None`)
  A time zone specifier used for returning `TIMESTAMP` types as
  timezone-aware native Python `datetime` objects.

  Different data types are supported. Available options are:

  - `datetime.timezone.utc`
  - `datetime.timezone(datetime.timedelta(hours=7), name="MST")`
  - `pytz.timezone("Australia/Sydney")`
  - `zoneinfo.ZoneInfo("Australia/Sydney")`
  - `+0530` (UTC offset in string format)

  The driver always returns timezone-"aware" `datetime` objects,
  with their `tzinfo` attribute set.

  When `time_zone` is `None`, the returned `datetime` objects are
  using Coordinated Universal Time (UTC), because CrateDB is storing
  timestamp values in this format.

  When `time_zone` is given, the timestamp values will be transparently
  converted from UTC to use the given time zone.
param timeout: |-
  (optional)
  define the retry timeout for unreachable servers in seconds
param username: the username in the database.
param verify_ssl_cert: |-
  if set to `False`, disable SSL server certificate verification.
  defaults to `True`
---

documentation:
make sure that only crate.exceptions are raised that are defined in
the DB-API specification

functions.name: crate.client.http._server_url
documentation:
Normalizes a given server string to an url

```pycon
>>> print(_server_url('a'))
http://a
>>> print(_server_url('a:9345'))
http://a:9345
>>> print(_server_url('https://a:9345'))
https://a:9345
>>> print(_server_url('https://a'))
https://a
>>> print(_server_url('demo.crate.io'))
http://demo.crate.io
```

functions.name: crate.client.http._update_pool_kwargs_for_ssl_minimum_version
documentation:
On urllib3 v2, re-add support for TLS 1.0 and TLS 1.1.

https://urllib3.readthedocs.io/en/latest/v2-migration-guide.html#https-requires-tls-1-2
